prompt:
Our ops CLIs share the little arg-parsing crate in this repo (rs-cliargs, src/lib.rs) — imperative builder, long flags only, and it's deliberately not clap; we own every line. It parses fine for flat tools, but the new `relay` tool needs git-style subcommands and we've been faking it with positionals. I've written the acceptance tests for the real thing in tests/subcommands.rs — please implement the feature in src/. Ground rule: tests/parser.rs is the shipped behavior and every test in it must still pass; don't touch anything under tests/, or Cargo.toml, or .gitignore.

Three pieces:

1) Subcommands. `Command::subcommand(self, sub: Command) -> Command` registers a nested Command (arbitrarily deep). When a command has subcommands, the first non-flag token must name one of them: parsing then hands EVERY remaining token to that subcommand (so a flag declared only on the parent is an unknown flag if it appears after the subcommand name, and vice versa — flags bind to the command level where they appear). A non-flag token that matches no subcommand fails with a new error variant `CliError::UnknownSubcommand { name: String }`, Display exactly "unknown subcommand 'NAME'". If the args run out before any subcommand token, parse succeeds and `Matches::subcommand()` is None. Accessor: `Matches::subcommand(&self) -> Option<(&str, &Matches)>` returning the chosen name and the nested matches. A value option still consumes the next token verbatim, so `--config fetch` is a value, never a dispatch. Commands with subcommands treat every non-flag token as a subcommand name (don't declare positionals on those).

2) `--` passthrough. On a literal `--` token, everything after it is collected verbatim — flag-looking tokens, later `--`s, anything — into `Matches::trailing(&self) -> &[String]` on the command being parsed at that moment (root or subcommand). Trailing tokens are NOT parsed as flags, NOT matched against subcommands, and do NOT fill declared positionals — declared positionals must already be satisfied before the `--`, else it's the usual MissingPositional error. With no `--` present, trailing() is empty. (Today a bare `--` falls through the positional path; no shipped test pins that, and the new behavior replaces it.)

3) Generated usage text: `Command::usage(&self) -> String`, deterministic, built only from the builder declarations, in declaration order (we will NOT sort anything alphabetically). Exact format: if about() was set, first the about line, then a blank line. Then "Usage: NAME", appending " [OPTIONS]" if the command declares any switch/option, then " <pos>" for each declared positional in order. If the command has subcommands, a second usage line follows: seven spaces (aligning under the name), then "NAME <COMMAND>". Then up to three sections, each preceded by exactly one blank line: "Commands:" (subcommand name + its about), then "Arguments:" ("<name>" + help), then "Options:" ("--name" for switches, "--name <VALUE>" for options, + help) — a section is omitted when it has no rows. Every row is two spaces, the left cell padded with spaces to the widest cell in ITS section, two spaces, then the help text (if a help text is empty, emit just the cell with no padding). Output ends with a newline. tests/subcommands.rs pins exact strings — treat those as the spec if I've mangled anything here.

Acceptance: `cargo test --offline` fully green — that's the new suite in tests/subcommands.rs AND every existing test in tests/parser.rs, no regressions.
